understanding of both John Deere and competitive products.

Job Role Description: PurposeThe Client Experience and Operations Manager is accountable for the complete client journey across all Green Leaf Group businesses and software platforms, with the primary purpose to ensure our client experience is revolutionary to healthcare - enabling us to retain clients long term and achieve the mission of Green Leaf Group.This includes ensuring every touchpoint - from initial enquiry and onboarding through service delivery, retention, and ongoing support - is meeting consistent, high-quality standards.As the Client Experience and Operations Manager for the Green Leaf Group, you are responsible for leading and optimising the operations of the Client Services, Logistics, and Pharmacy teams. You will also work collaboratively with senior management across the Green Leaf Group entities to develop and implement operational strategies, drive continuous improvement, and align departmental goals with the organisation’s vision and values.This pivotal role holds responsibility for aligning client experience, operational execution, and regulatory governance requirements. This includes designing, implementing, and continuously improving operational processes that support high quality patient care, protect clinicians and pharmacists, and maintain defensible standards under regulatory scrutiny.This role is designed to evolve over time:In the first phase, the Client Experience and Operations Manager will focus on operational stability, consistency, and risk reduction across Client Services, Logistics, and Pharmacy. This includes strengthening team capability, simplifying and embedding SOPs, improving booking accuracy, and ensuring complaints and exceptions are managed in a consistent and defensible manner.As operational foundations stabilise, the role will transition towards client journey redesign, systems and workflow optimisation, and longer-term experience strategy. This transition will be actively supported by the organisation and reviewed with the CEO at agreed milestones.Our Purpose, Vision and Core ValuesGreen Leaf Solutions is embarking on a groundbreaking mission to revolutionise healthcare. From redefining the essence of health to reimagining the significance of care in healthcare, we're committed to reshaping the experience of both our clients and our team members.We achieve this by upholding our values of:Genuine Commitment to CareAudacious attitudeNurture ownershipJump at opportunities for growthAltruistic Collaboration Decision Making and AuthorityThe Client Experience and Operations Manager holds delegated authority for operational decisions impacting the client journey across Client Services, Logistics, and Pharmacy, within agreed clinical, regulatory, and financial boundaries.Where client expectations, operational constraints, and clinical or regulatory requirements intersect, the role is responsible for balancing competing priorities and making sound, defensible decisions. Clear escalation pathways will be maintained to the CEO, Medical Director, and executive leadership team for matters involving patient safety, clinical governance, or material regulatory or reputational risk.Measures of SuccessSuccess in this role will be reflected through outcomes such as:Improved consistency and quality of the client and patient experienceReduced complaints and clearer root cause resolutionImproved booking accuracy and reduced operational reworkStronger alignment between client experience processes and clinical expectationsImproved staff engagement, capability, and retention within operational teamsReduced operational and regulatory risk through clearer SOPs and system improvements Specific Duties and ResponsibilitiesStrategic Planning and Execution:The role leads operational change across multiple teams and entities, driving standardisation, simplification, and continuous improvement in a growing and regulated healthcare environment. This includes:Leading teams through change with clarity, empathy, and accountability;Managing stakeholder expectations, including clinicians, senior leaders, and external partners;Setting and enforcing clear operational standards, including where difficult decisions are required;Balancing pace of improvement with sustainability and staff wellbeing.Develop and execute operational strategies that align with the company’s long-term vision and support efficient collaboration between Client Services, Logistics, and Pharmacy teams.Translate strategic goals into actionable operational plans that support business growth, efficiency, and exceptional client experiences.Ensure alignment of operations across Client Services, Logistics, and Pharmacy to deliver cohesive, high-quality outcomes.  Team Leadership and ManagementProvide strong leadership to the Client Services, Logistics, and Pharmacy teams, ensuring cohesive and high-performing operations.Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement, promoting accuracy, simplicity, efficiency, and innovation.Conduct regular performance evaluations and provide coaching, feedback, and mentoring to team members.Build team structure under your management to ensure business goals are met.Recruit, onboard, and develop high-performing staff in collaboration with the People & Culture team.Oversee the organisation of rosters and manage leave schedules to ensure adequate staffing levels and uninterrupted operations.Provide payroll information, including hours worked and leave records, to the People & Culture team in a timely and accurate manner.  Training and DevelopmentOversee design and implementation of induction and training programs that build team competency in areas such as client communication and regulatory compliance.Develop and manage the induction programme for all new staff to ensure consistency and quality of onboarding.Support ongoing professional development through mentoring, coaching, and performance feedback.Ensure staff induction and performance reviews are completed on schedule and properly recorded in accordance with company policy.Champion a learning culture across teams, encouraging innovation, accountability, and professional growth.  Performance Monitoring and ReportingDefine and monitor KPIs across Client Services, Logistics, and Pharmacy to measure efficiency, accuracy, and client satisfaction.Analyse performance data and prepare regular reports for senior management, highlighting successes, risks, and opportunities for improvement.Develop actionable insights from data to drive strategic decision-making and process optimisation.  Client Experience and Complaints ManagementEnsure all complaints are handled promptly, professionally, and appropriately, maintaining high standards of client satisfaction and compliance.Liaise with Product Strategy Manager for all product complaints and follow resolution processes as directed.Monitor complaint trends and collaborate with teams to implement improvements that prevent recurrence and enhance service quality.  Systems and Process Improvement and InnovationThe Client Experience Manager is accountable for the effective use and continuous improvement of systems supporting the client journey, including CRM, booking platforms, dispensing systems, and operational workflow tools. This includes:Defining operational requirements for systems and automation;Prioritising system improvements based on risk, efficiency, and client impact;Working collaboratively with technology, product, and external vendors;Reducing reliance on manual workarounds that introduce risk, inconsistency, or error.Identify opportunities to streamline workflows, automate manual processes, and enhance internal controls across all operational areas.Implement technology-driven solutions to improve efficiency and data accuracy.Lead continuous improvement projects, ensuring that all initiatives align with client satisfaction and operational excellence.  Facility Management:Ensure the wellbeing of staff and maintain a safe and comfortable environment.Manage and update pharmacy facilities to support operational needs and enhance the client experience.Oversee and coordinate resolution of technical and property issues, including hardware, Vemo systems, dispensary equipment, property maintenance and related infrastructure needs.  Compliance and Regulations:Stay informed on relevant laws and regulations governing healthcare operations.Ensure compliance with industry regulations, standards, and internal policies to mitigate risks and maintain integrity across operations. Financial Management and Budgeting:Manage operational budgets, monitoring expenditures and optimising resource allocation to achieve financial objectives.Identify cost-saving opportunities and implement measures to enhance profitability while maintaining operational standards.  Risk Management and Clinical Governance:The Client Experience Manager works closely with the Medical Director and Executive and Senior Leadership Teams to ensure all client experience processes, communications, and service models operate within established clinical governance frameworks. This includes:Ensuring operational practices support safe, appropriate prescribing and continuity of care.Avoiding operational or client driven pressure that could compromise clinical judgement.Embedding medicolegal requirements into booking pathways, patient communications, and service workflows.Supporting clinicians through clear, consistent, and defensible operational standards.Client experience initiatives must always align with clinical governance, patient safety, and regulatory obligations. Maintain operational readiness for regulatory review, audit, and external scrutiny. This includes ensuring clear documentation, consistent application of SOPs, and decision making that can be justified under regulatory and legal review.The role proactively identifies operational practices that could expose the organisation, clinicians, or directors to regulatory, medicolegal, financial, or reputational risk, and implements mitigations in collaboration with senior leadership.Implement contingency plans and crisis management protocols to ensure business continuity in the event of unforeseen circumstances or disruptions.  Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs):Develop, implement, and communicate SOPs to ensure consistent, high-quality care and services.Ensure all operational processes and SOPs are continually reviewed, simplified, and improved to enhance efficiency and the client experience.Monitor compliance with SOPs, provide training and support to staff, and continuously review and update procedures to maintain effectiveness and alignment with industry best practices.  Communication and Collaboration:Facilitate effective communication within and between departments and entities to enhance service delivery and client care.Participate in team meetings, providing updates and feedback from client interactions.Work collaboratively with team members to achieve business goals and improve client service strategies.  Health, Safety and Wellbeing: Comply with all the group’s policies, standards and procedures.Demonstrates employee health, safety and wellbeing is a high priority by incorporating health, safety and wellbeing into regular formal and informal group and individual meetings. Be familiar with emergency and evacuation procedures.Report all incidents, near misses and injuries immediately.Actively cooperate with any rehabilitation programme that is arranged to assist recovery from injury for yourself or for fellow employees.  Vision, Values, and Organisational Culture:Uphold the Green Leaf Group values and demonstrate these within your work.Participate as an active citizen within Green Leaf Group by playing an active role in culture, team events, and daily organisational life.  General Duties:Be punctual and work the hours and times specified.Prioritise workload to ensure work of the greatest importance to the business is undertaken with urgency and to a high standard.Support and help develop a positive workplace culture.Demonstrate excellent interpersonal communication skills.Responsibly manage all business resources within accountability levels.Undertake all duties and responsibilities outlined in this job description and all other duties as required by the business.Comply with all employment obligations.Promptly undertake to complete all reasonable and lawful instructions and directions given.Serve the business in good faith, promoting and protecting the business's best interests.During work time, and such other times as may be reasonably required, dedicate all effort to the execution and fulfillment of the duties, responsibilities, obligations, and instructions related to employment.Demonstrate through own actions a commitment to Health and Safety at work when undertaking work or observing others in the workplace.

Job Role Description:
The Parish Stewardship Coordinator is responsible for coordinating the Stewardship Ministries of the Catholic Parish of Bundaberg who are committed to implementing Stewardship as a way of life in the parish. 

Key Responsibilities
•	Work with and support the leaders of all Stewardship Ministries 
•	Work as a member of the Parish Leadership Team
•	Understand the organisation of parish ministries, under the banners of:
o	Liturgy and Prayer
o	Pastoral Care
o	Faith Formation and Education
o	Administration and Finance 
o	Outreach – to the people in the wider Bundaberg community 
•	Coordinate the renewal of all Stewardship Ministries every three years
•	Establish and Chair a Stewardship Committee to oversee the development of Stewardship as the “way of life” in the parish 
•	Understand and work towards achieving the goals of the Parish Stewardship Council 
•	Ensure all data relating to the Stewardship Ministries is updated regularly
•	Ensure that formation is available to all parishioners on the Christian principles of Stewardship
•	Perform any other duties as required and negotiated with the Parish Priest
Job Role Skills and Experience:
•	Formal qualifications in Stewardship, Theology, Scripture, Ministry and Mission (desirable) 
•	Intermediate competency in the use of Microsoft office suite, computer technology, and data management
•	Active participant in the Catholic Church community and/or lifestyle compatible with the values and teachings of the Church
•	Commitment to the Diocesan Vision Statement 
•	Commitment to Catholic Social Teachings
•	Information technology and social media literacy
•	Ability to provide a criminal history record check clear of convictions that relate to the inherit requirements of this position and obtain a Queensland Working with Children (Blue Card) Check, if requested 
•	Ensure ongoing disclosure of any criminal charges or allegations (current or historic), as soon as possible after such charges come to the employee’s attention and in accordance with the Personnel Screening Policy as amended from time to time. Failure to comply with this ongoing obligation to disclose may lead to disciplinary action, including dismissal
•	Current motor vehicle driver’s licence

Job Role Description: The Inwards Goods Quality Control Coordinator’s primary goal is to manage, execute, and safeguard the incoming material inspection process, ensuring that both Commercial Product and Contract Manufacturing production lines are continuously supplied with fully verified and compliant components. This role owns the raw material quality lifecycle at the point of receipt, coordinates defect containment and material sorting, maintains engineering "golden samples," and collaborates closely with the Senior Quality Assurance Engineer to process non-conformances and vendor replacements. Additionally, this position serves in a secondary capacity to provide critical operational coverage and support for the Production Quality Control Coordinator, ensuring seamless continuity across data entry, ESD control, and manufacturing documentation compliance.Job Responsibilities:1. Primary Focus: Inwards Goods Management & Material InspectionPriority & Flow Management: Reviews the Incoming Material List to identify, schedule, and execute inspection priorities, ensuring a reliable supply of materials to Commercial Product and Contract Manufacturing production lines. Order & Specification Verification: Reviews shipping details, order numbers, and quantities against engineering drawings, technical specifications, and purchase orders. AQL & Dimensional Inspections: Obtains reference drawings and samples to inspect incoming parts, executing critical dimension and finish checks using specialised measurement tools and instrumentation in strict adherence to AQL guidelines. Golden Sample Management: Manages and maintains the Manufacturing & Product Engineering (MPE) sample framework, ensuring "golden samples" are properly stored, controlled, and accessible as per company guidelines.Cross-functional Liaison: Liaises proactively with Engineering, Warehouse, and Production staff to facilitate efficient material inspection outcomes and minimise manufacturing downtime. 2. Primary Focus: Defect Containment, Sorting & NCR SupportIssue Containment & Sorting: Executes immediate inspection, isolation, and sorting of materials whenever a quality issue is identified or suspected on the floor or at receipt.Escalation & Communication: Communicates material defects, test failures, and variances directly to the Senior Quality Assurance Engineer for systemic evaluation. NCR & Replacement Coordination: Generates Rejected Material Advice notifications through the material management system and actively coordinates material replacements, supplier returns, and Non-Conformance Report (NCR) documentation. 3. Secondary Focus: Production Quality Coverage & SupportProduction Coordinator Coverage: Cross-trains to actively cover and support the Production Quality Control Coordinator during peak periods or absences. Quality Data Entry: Assists with standard quality data entry, capturing failure metrics and process logs to support graphical performance reporting. ESD Control Support: Assists with Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) compliance verifications and standard control measures across manufacturing zones.Production Documentation Verification: Participates in verifying production documentation compliance, reviewing manufacturing line logs and building records for completeness. 4. General & Regulatory ComplianceLegislative Compliance: Follows all Quality, Work Health & Safety (WHS), and Environment legislation, technical standards, and regulatory requirements. QA Evolution: Undertakes targeted tasks related to quality matters as directed by the department manager to support the continuous evolution of the company. Safety, Environment, Quality and Compliance ResponsibilitiesSafety Adherence: Ensures the safety of self, work colleagues, and the environment by following all workplace guidelines and safety criteria. Site Rules: Operates in complete accordance with company site rules, standard operating procedures, and established safety policies. Escalation: Promptly reports and escalates safety, environment, compliance, and quality concerns, incidents, or near misses.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Skills RequiredCommunication: Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to clearly document material defects and interface with engineering personnel. Problem Solving & Attention to Detail: Exceptional attention to detail with practical problem-solving and defect identification capabilities. Organisation & Time Management: Well-organised with strong time management skills and the ability to balance incoming volume spikes with production line demands. Collaboration: Strong team player with a customer-focused approach to supporting internal manufacturing cells. Experience & Person SpecificationCore Experience: Technically skilled with hands-on experience in warehouse, receiving, or manufacturing environments. Quality Frameworks: Prior experience operating within a structured Quality Management System (QMS) and utilising standard precision measuring equipment (e.g., callipers, micrometres) is highly regarded. Digital Literacy: Computer literate with standard proficiency in material management software, ERP systems, and basic spreadsheets for data entry.

Job Role Description: Position objectivesThis position is responsible for providing assistance to clients to be more independent in their own homes and the community, thereby enhancing their quality of life.  Home care services are provided which:   Promote each client’s capacity to live as independently as possible,   Take a person-centred approach to care, that promotes client’s wellness and active participation in the decisions about care; and  Provides timely, flexible and targeted services that are capable of maximising each client’s independence. Key responsibility areas Support clients to achieve and maintain wellbeing and independence as outlined in the individual support plan and in accordance with the Active Service Model. Responsible for the direct provision of the highest possible standard of a range of home and community care services to clients as determined by their assessed support plan including:   Assist clients with domestic assistance household/environmental tasks in relation to personal care and support such as maintaining a safe, hygienic living environment and undertaking a range of cleaning tasks. Provide a range of personal care tasks which enable the clients to maintain their desired standards of personal care and hygiene, such as assistance with mobility, bathing and toileting. Undertake a range of personal assistance/respite tasks as determined by the assessed support plan to support client independence and social support which may include:  Assist with meal planning and preparation.  Undertake shopping, banking and correspondence as required.  Escort clients to appointments or for shopping or leisure activities.  Assist with recreational activities appropriate to client preference. Provide a high level of customer service that is responsive, non-judgemental and professional to clients and carers at all times.  Monitor and report changes/concerns regarding client well-being and identified opportunities for enhancing client independence and social involvement.  Participate in continuous improvement and staff development processes as required by the Team Leader Positive Ageing. Maintain professional boundaries with clients at all times Perform, as directed, other duties that are within the limits of the incumbents’ skill, competence and training.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Qualifications and experienceEmployees will have satisfactorily completed structured training to one or more of the following levels:  Trade Certificate or equivalent.Completion of TAFE accredited/industry-based training courses or Knowledge and skills gained through on-the-job training commensurate with the requirements of the work in this position.  Selection criteriaMinimum Certificate III in Individual Support (or equivalent), and competencies in Level 2 First Aid and CPR, monitor medications and food handling. Experience and capacity to undertake general household duties, respite care and personal care tasks, which may at times be repetitive and/or physically demanding. Ability to communicate effectively, non-judgmentally and empathically with older people, people with disabilities and their carers. Awareness and support of the philosophies of healthy ageing and the Active Service Model.

Position Objective

The Community Services Program Manager is responsible for providing effective operations to provide quality services through compliance, management and support to employees within the Home-Based Care unit. 

The role of the Community Services Program Manager is to ensure the delivery of high-quality services that reflect best practice. This would include development and delivery of an appropriate range of confidential intense Children services with Cultural education and access to services to individuals, Kinship families, Aged care, Planned Activity groups, Carers and the community for promoting healthy lifestyles and healthy families with child safety programs.

The Community Services Program Manager will ensure high quality service provision through appropriate policy and program implementation, staff orientation, maintenance of supervision standards, staff appraisal and staff development systems, and area compliance.

Key Position Responsibilities

Primary Responsibilities


	Support the development and implementation of services within the team.
	Ensure that capacity of staff/client ratio is maintained within your program area
	Ensure implementation of the Referral process is followed by your program area
	Ensure data and required reporting to funding bodies is exported in a timely manner, with a report to management upon completion
	Ensure that effective communication between the Director of Operations, yourself and unit staff to ensure consistency and implementation across the Team. 
	Assist worker to meet contracting agreements for each child contracted to BDAC
	Review all court Reports and Case plan reports before endorsement and submission to Department of Human services
	Ensure Aged  Disability service programs are delivered


Community Education  Advocacy Support


	Support the development and facilitation of positive lifestyle change initiatives through planned activity group promotion and education opportunities for community
	Support the development of promotional materials and information
	Focus group/ feedback systems for guiding Improvement of services being delivered by program area
	Survey programs for feedback on the program area and future directions
	Promote BDAC programs and services to relevant external agencies and key stakeholders
	Ensure effective ongoing liaison with partner agencies, key stakeholders, services and personnel
	Seek additional funding to address gaps in the Home-Based Care program area
	Review and Update Fact sheets around your Program area

Job Role Description:
As the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Lutheran Care, you will report directly to the Lutheran Care Board and be responsible for providing strategic leadership in accordance with the organizations Christian ethos, particularly during times of societal change. You will be expected to utilize contemporary approaches in managing people, resources, and finances to deliver astute, sustainable, and innovative business solutions. Your primary objective will be to ensure that Lutheran Care has a recognized reputation for delivering high-quality services to people experiencing disadvantage or marginalization.

In addition, you will be responsible for developing and maintaining effective and influential relationships with a range of stakeholders, including government and community sector agencies, funders, and the Lutheran Church and its congregations in the provision of Lutheran Care services. Your role will be critical in ensuring that Lutheran Care remains relevant and impactful in the communities it serves, and that it maintains a strong reputation for excellence and integrity in all of its activities. As the CEO, you will be expected to lead with vision, integrity, and a deep commitment to the organizations mission and values.

A Seminarian is someone who is studying to become a Roman Catholic Priest. Their role includes: 
•	Academic study
Seminarians study Catholic theology, scripture, and pastoral ministry. They also receive a liberal arts college degree with a focus on philosophy and theology. 
•	Spiritual formation
Seminarians develop their prayer lives, participate in liturgical celebrations, and receive regular spiritual direction. 
•	Pastoral experience
Seminarians work with experienced priests and mentors to gain hands-on experience in pastoral ministry. This includes parish visits, hospital chaplaincy, youth ministry, and community outreach. 
•	Community life
Seminarians live in a shared community residence on the seminary grounds, where they enjoy meals together and engage in shared activities. 
•	Daily routine
Seminarians have a daily schedule of study, work, and prayer, as well as daily Mass, meetings, and workshops. 
Seminarians are encouraged to share their unique perspectives and experiences, and to cultivate their personal interests, talents, and hobbies. 
The four stages of seminary formation are: Propaedeutic, Discipleship, Configuration, and Vocational Synthesis.
Job Role Skills and Experience:
•	Deep commitment to the Catholic faith and adherence to its teachings
•	Capability to live a celibate lifestyle in accordance with church doctrine
•	Willingness to be relocated if needed by the diocese
•	Strong interpersonal skills and compassion
•	Physical and emotional stamina to meet the demands of pastoral care

Job Role Description: POSITION OVERVIEWThe Classroom Teacher at Padua College is a key contributor to the College’s mission to educate and inspire young men within a distinctly Franciscan educational community. The role is responsible for delivering high-quality, inclusive teaching and learning programs that promote academic excellence, student engagement, and holistic development. Working collaboratively with colleagues and leaders, the teacher designs and implements differentiated curriculum aligned to the College Learning Framework and QCAA requirements, ensuring responsiveness to the diverse needs, abilities, and aspirations of all students. Through contemporary, evidence-based pedagogies and effective assessment practices, the teacher supports strong student outcomes and continuous improvement in learning. Central to the role is a commitment to student wellbeing and pastoral care, fostering a safe, inclusive, and respectful environment where every student is known, valued, and supported. The teacher builds positive relationships with students, families, and the wider community, and contributes actively to the College’s pastoral structures and co-curricular life. This role also plays an important role in sustaining the College’s Catholic identity and Franciscan values (Joy, Courtesy, Humility, Simplicity, and Peace), modelling these through professional practice, relationships, and engagement in liturgical and service activities.More broadly, the Classroom Teacher contributes to the professional and community life of the College, maintaining high standards of professionalism, engaging in continuous professional learning, and supporting a collaborative culture focused on student success, wellbeing, and organisational excellence.KEY Job Role Description:
About The Program

The Loddon region is the location for an exciting and innovative partnership to provide earl intensive supports and intervention to young people entering care for the first time. The program aims to support reunification and/or stabilising placements.


The Loddon Care Hub is being funded by Department of Families, Fairness and Housing. This trial model brings together placement options, family services and outreach support to minimise the time a child or young person spends in care, and maximise the likelihood of successful reunification with family, by coordinating and enhancing:
 Placement options (foster and residential care);
 Therapeutic and cultural support;
 Family work and outreach support.
 Mental health and alcohol and other drugs support.


The Care Hub will provide early assessment, planning and wrap around supports by a single integrated team (formed from a consortium of organisations) for children and young people who are first time entrants into care.

The Position

The aim of the Key Practitioner role within the Care Hub is to undertake assessment, coordinate the care plan including the placement plan and be the one point of contact for the Care team.

The role will coordinate the multidisciplinary team in coordinating assessment, planning and support children and young people in the hub.

The Key Practitioner will provide quality interventions and services that are focused on Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families experiencing vulnerability as well as the safety and wellbeing of child/ren and young people.

This role will provide case coordination and a key point of contact for the Care Team and will work across the multi-disciplinary team including Child Protection to coordinate assessment, planning and support child/ren, young people and families.

What we are offering


	SCHADS Grade 6 Level 1
	Full Time
	12mths
	Access to Salary Packaging

The Head of Account Management is a strategic leadership role responsible for driving revenue growth and strengthening customer relationships across Australia and New Zealand by leading ELMO’s Account Management teams. Reporting to the Chief Customer Officer, this role refines and executes a market-leading go-to-market and account management strategy, with a strong focus on customer retention, upsell, and cross-sell within the existing customer base. 
The position leads and develops a high-performing team, sets and manages sales targets and budgets, leverages data and insights for forecasting and pipeline management, and collaborates closely with Sales, Marketing, Services, Customer Experience, and Support to deliver seamless, customer-centric outcomes. 
Key responsibilities include building trusted relationships with strategic accounts, integrating acquired customers, designing and improving the customer journey, managing escalations, and continuously enhancing account management processes to drive customer satisfaction, advocacy, and long-term growth.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Qualified candidates will hold a Bachelor’s degree (MBA preferred) and possess 8+ years of leadership experience within high-growth SaaS or HR-tech environments. As a proven leader of leaders, you have a track record of driving transformation initiatives that enhance customer satisfaction and deliver predictable revenue. You are a strategic, data-driven thinker with expertise in accurate forecasting, closing complex deals, and over-delivering on financial targets. Beyond your commercial acumen, you are a values-driven communicator committed to fostering high-performance cultures, building deep stakeholder trust, and championing customer advocacy to drive long-term loyalty.

Job Role Description: Faith Basis of AppointmentWe see the appointment of staff to Trinity Christian School as a ministry calling.The ethos, aims and objects of a Christian School are based on:> acceptance of the Lordship of Christ; and> acceptance of the Bible as the revealed Word of GodWithin Trinity Christian School these are expounded in the Doctrinal Basis.The Doctrinal Basis does not exhaust the extent of the beliefs of the School and its members. The Bible itself, as the inspired and infallible Word of God, that speaks with final authority concerning truth, morality, and the proper conduct of mankind, is the sole and final source of all that the School and its members believe.You must support the School in these areas and set an example of personal conduct and behaviour consistent with the School’s beliefs and ethos as outlined in documents made available to prospective employees prior to interview. Any appointment is conditional upon staff maintaining and demonstrating a continuing firm personal belief and lifestyle consistent with the School’s Doctrinal Basis and the other documents provided to staff, this being an essential condition of the appointment.A Learning Centred Christian SchoolAt Trinity Christian School we endeavour to make the link between our faith, what we do and how we teach. We call our students and school community to grow “loving hearts, enquiring minds and generous spirits”. At Trinity, how we teach must help our students in becoming who they were created to be in Christ. How we teach should also bring a Kingdom perspective, set students’ minds on things above, deliver meaning and show purpose.The pedagogy in our classrooms at Trinity should engage enquiring minds, build questioning, allow for grappling, exploring and reasoning. The students we teach should be given opportunities to grow and serve, so they may see who they are becoming and their purpose. As we undertake to grow our learners in both faith and learning, we must build in them the capacity to do so.As stated in our Strategic Priorities for 2024 – 2027, we have several key areas of focus that we are working toward:Christian Faith and GrowthThe spiritual nurturing of all individuals in the Christian faith, within a caring community of students, staff and families united by vision, mission and values. A passionate focus on realising the transformative power of the Gospel of Jesus, developing Biblical knowledge, and understanding and fulfilling the purpose of a Christian life.Excellence in Education and LearningAll students actively engaged, growing and thriving as learners in a learning centred school. Parent partnership and student agency enhanced and supported. A commitment to educational excellence with the creation and support of new ways of learning, varied student pathways and models of education, to meet the needs of each learner. Community and BelongingA movement by all students, staff and families from joining and attending the school to belonging and actively investing in the Trinity community. Connection and relationship developed and strengthened with those in the School’s history as well as future community members.Connecting with the World and Shaping TomorrowOngoing design and formation of our 2030 learning environment for the community now and in the future. A growth in our reach and impact on Christian Education nationally and globally, including shaping a reputation as a centre for staffing development and excellence.Benefits of Working at Trinity Christian SchoolTrinity Christian School is an employer that promotes staff excellence and values the benefit that our professional team bring to the school. In addition to the highly competitive remuneration and conditions as set out in the MEA we offer the following additional benefits to our staff across the school including:> Attractive salary packaging arrangements.> School fee concessions.> Generous annual Staff Development Award program.> Access to an Employee Assistance Program.> Access to annual influenza vaccinations.Other Essential RequirementsEmployment ConditionsEmployment will be subject to the conditions of the current ACT Christian School General Staff Multi-Enterprise Agreement 2021 or its replacement. The salary and classification for this position will be dependent on the qualifications and experience of the successful applicant.A Committed, Practicing ChristianApplicants must be able to demonstrate a personal faith and commitment to the Lord Jesus Christ and a lifestyle consistent with that faith, including an active commitment to a Christian fellowship.Right to Work In AustraliaApplicants must be Australian Citizens or hold a current and valid working visa. The School will not consider sponsorship nor support a visa application process. Please do not submit an application if you cannot meet this requirement.Working With Vulnerable People CheckIn keeping with the School’s policy and legislative requirements, any offer of employment is subject to you providing a satisfactory Working With Vulnerable People check prior to commencing employment and maintaining this for the duration of your employment.AcknowledgementThe requirements of this job description are intended to describe the general nature and responsibility of the work in this role. These statements are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks and skills required of the job. Employees will also be required to follow any other job-related instructions and school policies, and to perform other job-related duties requested by their Manager to support the School’s compliance with its legislative obligations. The Manager may, through consultation with the employee, vary the responsibilities of the position temporarily as required, but within the skills and responsibility levels appropriate to the position.Further InformationAll enquiries will be treated confidentially. Job Role Description: The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is a senior executive responsible for ensuring the effective implementation of the organisation’s strategic direction through robust operational and workforce leadership. Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), the COO provides oversight across Operations and Human Resources, aligning service delivery with the organisation’s purpose, values, and strategic plan. The COO is accountable for high-quality and sustainable service delivery, workforce planning and development, and strong business management practices. They play a pivotal role in governance support, working in partnership with the CEO and Board to ensure compliance, risk management, and organisational performance.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Ability to think long-term, anticipate sector reforms, and align operational plans with strategic goals.  Skilled in leading transformation, managing workforce change, and building adaptability across teams. Builds trusted relationships with staff, the CEO, the Board, and external stakeholders; able to influence and negotiate effectively. Exercises sound judgment, balancing risk and opportunity, and ensures decisions are aligned with organisational values and priorities.  Strong focus on efficiency, compliance, and continuous improvement to ensure high-quality service delivery.  Inspires and develops people, fostering a positive, safe, and inclusive workplace culture.  Excellent communicator, able to represent the organisation professionally, clearly, and persuasively.  Models ethical behaviour, accountability, and commitment to child safety, equity, and organisational values. Understanding of employment legislation, industrial instruments, and policy frameworks to ensure compliance and best practice.  Postgraduate qualification in Business Administration, Management, Human Resources, or a related field; or equivalent senior executive experience. Extensive leadership experience in complex service-based organisations, ideally within early childhood education. Demonstrated experience in strategic and operational planning, workforce management, and service delivery oversight.  Proven success in managing large teams, including HR functions, and driving organisational change and improvement.  Strong financial and business management skills, including budget development, resource allocation, and sustainability planning. Demonstrated knowledge of governance frameworks and experience working with a Board and/or executive leadership teams. Proven ability to build effective partnerships with government, community, and sector stakeholders.

Job Role Description:
Role purpose
The Head of Finance oversees finance, property, and procurement managing AICD’s budget development, forecasting, financial statement preparation and functions including payroll, accounts payable and accounts receivable, procurement and property management.

Key responsibilities 
Finance and Payroll
•	Directs all aspects of financial and management reporting, ensuring accuracy, timeliness and
       Relevance.
•	Consults with and advises senior business stakeholders, assisting them to achieve their business   
       Outcomes.
•	Oversight of the production of budgets and forecasts that align to the AICD strategic plans.
•	Embeds strong financial control practices across the AICD.
•	Oversees the preparation of statutory accounts and other statutory obligations.
•	Influences the financial direction and performance of the AICD.
•	Sets the strategic direction for finance systems, processes, people and value proposition.
•	Direct oversight for all payroll processing and associated obligations.
•	Anticipates needs of the Audit and Risk Committee (ARC) through the Committee charter and acts on instructions from the Committee.
•	Makes recommendations to the Executive and Board in respect of finance policy.
•	Maintains a close working relationship with the external auditors.

Investments
•	Anticipates needs of the ARC through the Committee charter and acts on instructions from the Committee.
•	Monitors the investment strategy in accordance with the AICD Investment Policy.

Property
•	Supporting the development and implementation of the AICD’s Property Strategy. 
•	Management of the property budget. 
•	Facilitating the negotiations of office leases including new, renewals, surrenders and relocations.
•	Overseeing the management of property projects, including selection and development of property plans, fit outs, timelines and budgets. 
•	Managing relationships with key internal and external property stakeholders. 
•	Ensuring national standards for office services across the AICD including scheduled maintenance, utilities, waste, cleaning, storage, security systems and employee services including lockers and desk management services. 
•	Providing recommendations relating to risk in areas of building management and WH&S. 
•	Ensuring the location, design and fit out of each office is fit for purpose.
•	Ensuring each location is a safe and attractive work environment. 

Procurement
•	Ensuring the Procurement and Vendor Management Policy is adhered to across AICD and is regularly maintained. 
•	Overseeing complex and large vendor negotiations with new and existing vendor suppliers. Instilling a long-term view of savings through effective vendor and procurement practices, including the standardisation of products and services to drive lower cost of ownership, along with ensuring pricing and value is competitively benchmarked. 
•	Collaborating with legal and risk representatives to develop and insert appropriate legal protections in contracts, as warranted. 

Stakeholder Management
•	Support and advise stakeholders, including internal groups such as the Audit and Risk Committee, Executive Committee, and Senior Leadership Team, and external stakeholders such as AICD’s External Auditor, ACNC, banks, investment advisors, and office landlords. 

Team Management
•	Plan, control and execute budgets where applicable using knowledge, skills, and resources to meet budget. 
•	Lead, develop, motivate and coach employees to enhance their performance, capability and development.
•	Support employees in performance development including completing annual performance appraisals to achieve their career goals.
•	Manage poor performance in accordance with AICD’s policies and procedures. Ensure records of actions taken of any performance action are well documented.
•	Oversee regular reviews of systems/processes to implement continuous improvements.

AICD Contribution
•	Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. 
•	Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support AICD’s strategic plan as required. 
•	Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to AICD for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. 

Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis
Job Role Skills and Experience:
Essential knowledge, skills and experience
•	Tertiary qualification in Finance
•	CA / CPA
•	Strong analytical, conceptual and structured thinking
•	Anticipates and responds to change
•	Strong technical accounting knowledge
•	Expert in managing operational accounting issues matters
•	Inspirational leadership
•	Exceptional verbal and written communication skills
•	Strong understanding of finance and analytical systems
•	A strategic and commercial thinker
•	Track record in leading medium sized teams
•	Completion of appropriate Accounting Degree

Competencies                                                       
•	Analysing
•	Writing & Reporting
•	Planning & Organising
•	Entrepreneurial & Commercial Thinking
•	Deciding & Initiating Action 
•	Working with People

Job Role Description:
The Supply Chain Manager oversees logistics and operations, ensuring efficiency and compliance while managing supplier relationships and inventory.
Logistics Management: Oversee the entire supply chain process, including sourcing, production, inventory management, and distribution of goods.
Supplier Coordination: Develop and maintain strong relationships with suppliers to ensure timely delivery and quality of materials.
Performance Monitoring: Track and report on supply chain performance metrics, ensuring compliance with Australian regulations and industry standards.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to assess performance and identify areas for improvement.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills to effectively collaborate with internal teams and external partners.
Strong leadership and strategic planning skills to manage teams and drive performance.

Job Role Description:
The Aboriginal Education Worker (AEW) role is to support Aboriginal people to engage in our programs and to access the services we provide in financial wellbeing, and through assisting other staff to deliver higher level Financial Wellbeing services. The AEW will provide on-ground support to help people understand western concepts relating to money and finances, and to make informed choices about their money and build money management skills. 

They will also assist with the development and delivery of budgeting and financial literacy information sessions and workshops and support our team to ensure communication is culturally sensitive. This role requires regular travel to provide services to Aboriginal remote communities within Central Australia, you will be required to travel to remote communities for a minimum of 2 weeks per month, at times you will be travelling remotely for a block of 2 weeks.
